betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Automated Labor: Robots§f

§aDeploy programmable mobile workers that can mine, farm, build, and level up!§f
Robots are fully autonomous, mobile computers. They execute scripts to interact with the environment, harvest blocks, and manage inventories.

§6Movement & Flight Constraints§f:
§b• Movement Cost§f: Moving a robot forwards/backwards/up/down costs §e15 energy§f.
§b• Turn Cost§f: Rotating the robot 90 degrees costs §e2.5 energy§f.
§b• Flight Height Limit§f: By default, robots can only hover up to §e8 blocks§f above a solid surface. Attempting to move higher will fail.
§b• Hover Upgrades§f: Equip Hover Upgrades to lift this restriction:
  §e• Tier 1 Hover Upgrade§f: Max flight height §e64 blocks§f.
  §e• Tier 2 Hover Upgrade§f: Max flight height §e256 blocks§f.

§6Leveling & Experience (XP)§f:
As robots perform successful actions (breaking blocks, placing blocks, attacking mobs), they gain experience. Robots can level up from §eLevel 1 to 30§f, granting major bonuses:
§b• Energy Buffer§f: Base buffer is §e20,000 energy§f. Each level adds §a+5,000 capacity§f, reaching §e170,000 total§f at max level.
§b• Tool Efficiency§f: Robots use tools extremely efficiently. The base chance of a tool losing durability on use is only §e10% (0.1)§f. Each level adds a §a+1% efficiency bonus§f (making tools last even longer).
§b• Harvest Speed§f: Breaking speed increases by §a+2% per level§f.

§6Charging & Maintenance§f:
Robots do not self-generate power by default. 
To charge them, place a §bCharger§f block connected to your power grid. The Charger transfers up to §e100 energy per tick§f to adjacent robots.
§eNote§f: The charge rate scales with redstone signal strength. A maximum strength signal (15) charges at full speed, while a signal of 8 charges at half speed.
